summary.intent: Fix a broken RPM build in the release workflow and add an HTML changelog page to the moltis.org website for new releases.
summary.outcome: Fixed the RPM build TOML string quoting bug in release.yml, created a changelog build script (website/scripts/build-changelog.mjs), integrated it into the release workflow's deploy step, and committed everything together.

repo: website/install.sh is a separate copy of the root install.sh and must be kept in sync — it is the actual file served at moltis.org/install.sh
The website is deployed as static files (likely Cloudflare Pages/Workers); generated HTML files must be committed to the repo, not just built at runtime
The release workflow's update-deploy-tags job uses the GitHub API to create commits with specific blobs, requiring Node.js to be available for build scripts
cargo generate-rpm --set-metadata expects inline TOML, so version strings must be quoted (version = "20260327.02") or they parse as floats

code.finding: RPM --set-metadata version field must be TOML-quoted string; unquoted date-style versions like 20260327.02 are parsed as floats, causing 'Field version must be string' error
Used as the template pattern for the new build-changelog.mjs script
Build scripts updated to include changelog generation step

workflow: When debugging CI failures, check the specific failed step logs rather than the top-level job summary
cargo-generate-rpm's --set-metadata flag requires careful TOML type awareness for version fields

friction: CI failure on RPM build required multiple bash commands to drill down to the actual error message
Shell working directory drift (cd into website/ during testing) caused the final commit to fail from the wrong directory, requiring a second commit attempt from the project root
The release workflow's blob-based GitHub API commit approach added complexity to integrating the changelog build step
open_items: Verify the re-triggered release workflow (20260327.02) succeeds end-to-end with the TOML fix and changelog integration
Confirm the generated changelog/index.html is correctly picked up and deployed by the Cloudflare Pages/Workers pipeline
